items.title: Autobiographical Memory Disturbances in Schizophrenia and the Psychosis Continuum: Integrating New Insights
items.author: Mélissa C. Allé, Dorthe Berntsen
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Most studies on autobiographical memory in the psychosis continuum have focused exclusively on the voluntary (strategic) retrieval of past events. Findings have identified significant memory deficits in terms of impaired retrieval, reduced recollective ...</article>

items.title: Nature Relatedness Usefully Links Well-Being, the Environment, and Sustainability
items.author: John M. Zelenski, Paul K. Lutz, Holli-Anne Passmore, Elizabeth K. Nisbet
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Environmental and mental-health problems intersect unpleasantly in climate anxiety, yet they may share a common salve via connecting with nature. We review research on nature relatedness (people’s subjective sense of connection with nature) that shows ...</article>

items.title: Emotional Acculturation: Emotions as a Pathway to Social Integration
items.author: Heejung S. Kim, Batja Mesquita
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. This article reviews recent research that examines how emotional processes change in response to exposure to new cultures and how successful changes in emotional processes play crucial roles in immigration outcomes. items.title: Event Segmentation Interventions Improve Memory for Naturalistic Events
items.author: Maverick E. Smith, Jeffrey M. Zacks
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. People segment ongoing experience into meaningful chunks that support new learning and long-term memory. We synthesize evidence showing that scaffolding segmentation improves memory, possibly by reducing interference. These findings highlight the role of ...</article>
